reasoning:
On February 1, 2026, Richard Rapport's FIDE ranking is #16 according to the Wikipedia FIDE Rankings table. The FIDE rankings are updated exactly once a month (on the 1st of the month). Therefore, by February 8, 2026, the rankings on Wikipedia will remain identical to the February 1 list. Since his ranking will be mathematically exactly the same (#16), it perfectly satisfies the criteria "as high or higher than their ranking on 2026-02-01" (equality qualifies). That gives a virtual certainty (98%) for the first date. 

For the second date (March 3, 2026), FIDE will have published the new March 2026 rankings, and the Wikipedia page will have been updated to reflect them. Whether Rapport will remain at #16 or improve depends on tournament performances in February (both his own, if he plays, and those of the players immediately above and below him, such as Dominguez, Duda, Le Quang Liem, and Aronian). In a random walk of rankings among similarly-rated players, there is typically a ~50% chance of retaining/improving rank vs dropping, but players who are inactive face a slightly higher risk of being leapfrogged than of passively moving up (though both happen). Since no major definitive tournament results for his immediate peers could be confirmed for February, the base rate heavily applies, putting the probability near 48% due to the symmetric likelihood of minor rating fluctuations.
